children.name: ParseCommand
children.purpose: Extract and validate the command field from the input JSON
children.behavior: Parse the input JSON to extract the 'command' field and validate it is one of the allowed commands (send, history, create_channel, join). Return the command and remaining payload.
postconditions: Command is one of the allowed values
guarantees: Returns error if command is missing or invalid

constraints.description: Command must be one of: send, history, create_channel, join

acceptance_criteria.description: Valid JSON with valid command returns command and payload

children.name: SendMessage
children.purpose: Handle sending a message to a channel
children.behavior: Validate that the user is a member of the specified channel, then store the message in the messages data store with timestamp and user info.
preconditions: User is member of the channel
postconditions: Message is stored in messages data store
guarantees: Returns error if user not in channel

call_inputs.name: payload
call_inputs.description: Contains channel, user, and message content

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check if user is a member of the channel

global_vars.description: Check if user is a member of the channel

data_operations.description: Check if user is a member of the channel

constraints.description: User must be a member of the channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Valid message from channel member is stored successfully

children.name: GetHistory
children.purpose: Retrieve message history for a channel
children.behavior: Validate that the user is a member of the channel, then fetch the last 100 messages from the messages data store for that channel, ordered by timestamp.
preconditions: User is member of the channel
guarantees: Returns error if user not in channel

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check if user is a member of the channel

global_vars.description: Check if user is a member of the channel

data_operations.description: Check if user is a member of the channel

constraints.description: User must be a member of the channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Channel member retrieves message history successfully

children.name: CreateChannel
children.behavior: Check that the channel name does not already exist, then create a new channel entry in the channels data store with the creator as the first member.
preconditions: Channel name does not already exist
postconditions: Channel is created and creator is added as member
guarantees: Returns error if channel already exists

call_inputs.name: payload
call_inputs.description: Contains channel name and creator user

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check if channel exists, then create new channel

global_vars.description: Check if channel exists, then create new channel

data_operations.description: Check if channel exists, then create new channel

acceptance_criteria.description: New channel is created successfully with creator as member

children.name: JoinChannel
children.purpose: Add a user to an existing channel
children.behavior: Check that the channel exists and the user is not already a member, then add the user to the channel's member list in the channels data store.
preconditions: User is not already a member
postconditions: User is added to channel's member list
guarantees: Returns error if channel does not exist or user already member

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check if channel exists and user not member, then update member list

global_vars.description: Check if channel exists and user not member, then update member list

data_operations.description: Check if channel exists and user not member, then update member list

constraints.description: User must not already be a member

governance_notes: ParseCommand is a pure function with no resource access. All other children access the 'channels' and/or 'messages' data stores as specified in their internal_leaf_accesses. global_vars and data_operations are synchronized for each child. No deliberate exceptions exist.

resource_audit.signature_preservation_detail: All signatures remain unchanged from Stage 2. ParseCommand takes 'input', all others take 'payload'. No resources appear in signatures.
resource_audit.internal_leaf_mapping_detail: SendMessage maps channels.read and messages.write. GetHistory maps channels.read and messages.read. CreateChannel maps channels.read_write. JoinChannel maps channels.read_write. ParseCommand has no internal_leaf_accesses.
resource_audit.global_vars_data_operations_sync_detail: For each child, global_vars and data_operations contain identical entries. ParseCommand has empty arrays for both. All other children have matching entries for each resource they access.
resource_audit.coverage_detail: All resources mentioned in internal_leaf_accesses are covered in global_vars and data_operations. No resources are missing. ParseCommand correctly has no resource access.
